pulse: The Energy sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 11.1x and a P/E of 17.9x, both in line with the broader market's P/E of 21.6x, but reflect a weaker growth profile with median revenue growth at -1.3%. Notably, COP trades at a significant discount with an EV/EBITDA of 6.5x and a P/E of 17.9x, while CVX commands a premium at 28.2x P/E despite revenue declines of -8.2%, indicating a widened valuation spread and potential overvaluation in large-cap names. As earnings season reveals slowing revenue growth across the sector, investors may want to reassess exposure to high-valuation entities like CVX and focus on names with stronger margins and cash flow yields, such as EOG, as macroeconomic headwinds could pressure valuations further.
